original_abstract: In recent years, the field of artificial intelligence (AI) has undergone a profound paradigm shift driven by the transformative capabilities of generative models across both supervised and unsupervised learning frameworks. Generative AI has achieved state-of-the-art results in addressing complex real-world challenges, spanning image-to-image translation, medical diagnostics, multimodal synthesis, and natural language processing. This paper provides a systematic review and analysis of recent technical advancements in generative modeling, offering an in-depth discussion of domain-specific architectures and their practical implementations. We specifically highlight the revolutionary impact of Large Language Models (LLMs) and diffusion-based image synthesis, alongside emerging interdisciplinary applications. The primary contribution of this work is a coherent synthesis of the current landscape, weaving together contemporary breakthroughs to provide a roadmap for the future trajectory of the field. Finally, we address the critical importance of Responsible AI principles, examining the ethical considerations necessary to ensure the sustainable growth and societal integration of generative technologies.
humanized_abstract: Over the last few years, there has been a significant paradigm shift in the field of Artificial Intelligence (AI) using generative models in both supervised and unsupervised learning. The breakthroughs in generative models has led to the state of the art solutions to several real-world problems using image-to-image translation, image classification, multimedia synthesis and text analysis. This work presents a systematic review of the recent advancements in generative models along with the associated domain specific architectures, and the implementation for the various applications. In addition, this work highlights the recent breakthroughs of Large Language Models (LLMs) and diffusion-based image synthesis and their interdisciplinary applications. The main contribution of this work is the synthesis of the present works done in the field of generative models and connecting the recent works to showcase the future direction of this research area. Finally, this work highlights the need and importance of the Responsible AI and the ethics associated with the generative models and the future growth and acceptability of the generative models.
